name: Zucchini Stuffed with Lamb and Mint
recipeIngredient: 1 bunch fresh mint leaves, picked but left whole
recipeInstructions:
1. To Make Tomato Sauce: Saute 1 diced Spanish onion and 4 sliced garlic cloves in olive oil over medium heat for about 10 minutes, or until translucent but not browned. Add 3 ounces virgin thyme leaves and 1 shredded carrot, cook for an additional 5 minutes. Stir in 2 cans crushed tomatoes (28 oz each) and bring to a boil. Lower the heat to just bubbling and simmer for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally. Season with salt to taste and set aside.
2. To Make Stuffed Zucchini: Preheat the oven to 450 degrees Fahrenheit. Trim and cut 4 zucchinis in half lengthwise. Remove the center of each half using a spoon or melon baller, leaving about a 1/4-inch around the edges and at the ends intact.
3. Roughly chop the excess zucchini flesh and set aside. In a large sauté pan, heat olive oil until it just smokes. Add the diced onion and sliced garlic, cooking over medium-high heat for about 6 to 7 minutes until softened.
4. Add the zucchini pieces and crumbled lamb to the pan, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is cooked through (about 10 minutes). Remove from heat and let the mixture cool slightly.
5. In a large mixing bowl, combine the cooled lamb mixture with 2 eggs, half of the chopped mint leaves, and the tomato sauce. Mix well.
6. Season both the stuffing mixture and the zucchini boats with salt and pepper. Stuff each zucchini boat generously with the lamb mixture, then pile it high.
7. Place the stuffed zucchini in an oven-proof baking dish large enough to hold them. Sprinkle with bread crumbs and bake for 20 to 25 minutes until crisp on top. Serve warm or at room temperature with a chicory salad, garnished with remaining mint leaves.
